channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jerry 'Ice Man' Butler, soul singer whose hits included 'Only the Strong Survive,' dies at 85
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jerry Butler, a premier soul singer of the 1960s and after whose rich, intimate baritone graced such hits as "For Your Precious Love," "Only the Strong Survive" and "Make It Easy On Yourself," has died at age 85.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol on assault charge after press conference
item.description: Former Proud Boys national leader Enrique Tarrio has been arrested near the U.S. Capitol on a charge that he assaulted a woman protesting a gathering attended by Tarrio and others who received presidential pardons for crimes stemming from the Jan. 6, 2021, riot in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since his arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare in New York City and leading authorities on a five-day search was in court Friday for the first time since his December arraignment on state murder and terror charges.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida woman who told insurance compant 'delay, deny, depose' has charge dropped
item.description: The charge against Lakeland woman accused of threatening her health insurance company was dropped on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KREX 5

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Los Angeles DA to give update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney will give an update Friday on the case of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who are seeking release after their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: Six weeks after devastating wildfires, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the city’s fire chief amid a public rift over preparations for the fires and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis isn't out of danger but his condition isn't life-threatening, medical team says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis’ complex respiratory infection isn't life-threatening but he's not out of danger, his medical team said Friday, as the 88-year-old pontiff marked his first week in the hospital battling pneumonia in both lungs — along with bacterial, viral and fungal infections on top of his
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Los Angeles district attorney to update public in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles district attorney will give an update Friday on the case of Lyle and Erik Menendez, brothers who are seeking release after their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: A federal judge has adjourned the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department's request to drop charges against the Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colorado avalanche victim rescued in dramatic video
item.description: A Colorado avalanche victim was rescued in Vail Pass after spending more than an hour underneath the snow, the Summit County Sheriff’s Office said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge allows Trump's mass firings of federal workers to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge in Washington has allowed President Donald Trump’s mass firings of federal workers to move forward. U.S. District Judge Christopher Cooper decided Thursday he could not grant a motion from unions representing the workers to temporarily block the layoffs.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Funeral held for 2 Detroit kids who died in cold while family gets promise of a home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A group has stepped forward to provide a home for surviving family members of two Detroit children who died in a cold van in a casino parking garage, an official said. A funeral was held Thursday for Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: Trader Joe’s has issued a recall of its Organic Acai Bowls over the potential that the product may contain plastic, the company announced late last week. A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product had been removed from sale as of Feb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: IRS lays off 6,700 employees, torpedoing Democratic enforcement boost
item.description: The IRS fired 6,700 employees Thursday, a government official told NewsNation, the sister television network of The Hill. The employees were designated as probationary, meaning they were working for the agency on a trial basis prior to becoming full staff members.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, WGN 9

item.title: Snowy roads lead to hundreds of Virginia and North Carolina crashes as Arctic air brings record cold
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Roads in Virginia and North Carolina remained treacherous Thursday as unusually heavy snow led to hundreds of accidents, including crashes on an interstate that involved more than 50 vehicles.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell, Milk Bar team up again for a sweet new treat
item.description: Taco Bell and Milk Bar are teaming up once again for a new sweet treat. The brands announced the launch of the new Milk Bar Birthday Cake Churros, a little treat filled with birthday cake frosting and topped with bright pink confetti sprinkles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump Education pick Linda McMahon clears committee, heads to full Senate
item.description: Linda McMahon, President Trump’s pick for Education secretary, passed the Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee on Thursday morning, sending her confirmation to the full Senate.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Department of Homeland Security fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump's administration as the White House and its Department of Government Efficiency fire both new and career workers, tell agency leaders to plan for “large-scale reductions
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
